text:
Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Ruby. She longed to be an artist. She would spend her days dreaming about painting grand canvasses full of mysterious forests, twinkling stars, and charming cats.

Ruby's parents were busy professionals, so she spent her days roaming the city corners. She'd sneak into stores to look at art books, often stealing little sketches she drew in her sketchbook. Those drawings weren't just pretty; they told stories, showcased Ruby's imagination.

Feeling lonely sometimes, Ruby cherished secret visits to a local park near her home. It was there she stumbled upon an old, charming studio. The door was weathered, almost like an old friend. Inside, rows upon rows of beautiful paintings filled the room.

One afternoon as the sun painted the sky with hues of orange and pink, Ruby crept closer. She discovered an old painting, vibrant and alive. It wasn't just any picture—it was of a cozy park on a winter's evening
